Interface ProverCore<P extends ProofObject<G>, G extends @Nullable ProofGoal<G>>

Type Parameters:
P - type of proof object, which extends ProofObject.
G - The type of proof goal, which extends ProofGoal.
All Known Implementing Classes:
AbstractProverCore, ApplyStrategy, DefaultProver
public interface ProverCore<P extends ProofObject<G>, G extends @Nullable ProofGoal<G>>
The ProverCore interface defines the core operations for managing and executing automated proof strategies in the KeY framework. Implementations of this interface provide mechanisms to start proof searches, manage listeners for proof tasks, and handle interruption or resource cleanup.

  • Field Summary

    Fields
    Modifier and Type
    Field
    Description
    static final String
    PROCESSING_STRATEGY
    A constant used by some listeners to identify that a proof macro is running.

    addProverTaskObserver(ProverTaskListener observer)
    Adds a listener to monitor proof task events.

    clear()
    Clears all resources and state associated with the current proof session.
    boolean
    hasBeenInterrupted()
    Indicates whether the last proof run was interrupted due to an InterruptedException.
    void
    removeProverTaskObserver(ProverTaskListener observer)
    Removes a listener that monitors proof task events.
    ProofSearchInformation<P,G>
    start(P proof, G goal)
    Starts a proof search for the given goal using the specified proof instance and the strategy settings provided by the proof instance.
    ProofSearchInformation<P,G>
    start(P proof, ImmutableList<G> goals)
    Starts a proof search for a list of goals using the specified proof instance and strategy settings provided by the proof instance.

    Starts a proof search for a list of goals using the specified proof instance, with configurable options for maximum steps, timeout, and early termination.
    ProofSearchInformation<P,G>
    start(P proof, ImmutableList<G> goals, Object stratSet)
    Starts a proof search for a list of goals using the specified proof instance and the provided strategy settings.

  • Field Details

    • PROCESSING_STRATEGY

      static final String PROCESSING_STRATEGY
      A constant used by some listeners to identify that a proof macro is running.
      See Also:
      • Constant Field Values

  • Method Details

    • start

      ProofSearchInformation<P,G> start(P proof, G goal)
      Starts a proof search for the given goal using the specified proof instance and the strategy settings provided by the proof instance.
      Parameters:
      proof - The ProofObject representing the proof instance.
      goal - The ProofGoal to prove.
      Returns:
      An ProofSearchInformation object containing information about the performed work, such as the number of rules applied.

    • start

      ProofSearchInformation<P,G> start(P proof, ImmutableList<G> goals)
      Starts a proof search for a list of goals using the specified proof instance and strategy settings provided by the proof instance.
      Parameters:
      proof - The ProofObject representing the proof instance.
      goals - An ImmutableList of ProofGoal objects to prove.
      Returns:
      An ProofSearchInformation object containing information about the performed work, such as the number of rules applied.

    • start

      ProofSearchInformation<P,G> start(P proof, ImmutableList<G> goals, Object stratSet)
      Starts a proof search for a list of goals using the specified proof instance and the provided strategy settings.
      Parameters:
      proof - The ProofObject representing the proof instance.
      goals - An ImmutableList of ProofGoal objects to prove.
      stratSet - The strategy settings to use during the proof search.
      Returns:
      An ProofSearchInformation object containing information about the performed work, such as the number of rules applied.

    • start

      ProofSearchInformation<P,G> start(P proof, ImmutableList<G> goals, int maxSteps, long timeout, boolean stopAtFirstNonCloseableGoal)
      Starts a proof search for a list of goals using the specified proof instance, with configurable options for maximum steps, timeout, and early termination.
      Parameters:
      proof - The ProofObject representing the proof instance.
      goals - An ImmutableList of ProofGoal objects to prove.
      maxSteps - The maximum number of rule applications to perform.
      timeout - The maximum duration (in milliseconds) to perform the proof search.
      stopAtFirstNonCloseableGoal - If true, the proof search stops at the first encountered non-closeable goal.
      Returns:
      An ProofSearchInformation object containing information about the performed work, such as the number of rules applied.

    • addProverTaskObserver

      void addProverTaskObserver(ProverTaskListener observer)
      Adds a listener to monitor proof task events.
      Parameters:
      observer - The ProverTaskListener to add.

    • removeProverTaskObserver

      void removeProverTaskObserver(ProverTaskListener observer)
      Removes a listener that monitors proof task events.
      Parameters:
      observer - The ProverTaskListener to remove.

    • clear

      void clear()
      Clears all resources and state associated with the current proof session. This is typically called when a proof obligation is abandoned to prevent memory leaks and ensure that references to the proof are reset.

    • hasBeenInterrupted

      boolean hasBeenInterrupted()
      Indicates whether the last proof run was interrupted due to an InterruptedException. This flag allows detection of interruptions even when the exception has been swallowed by the system.
      Returns:
      true if the last run was interrupted, false otherwise.
